UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores personal information, role, and preferences for an application user. This entity supports different user types: Elderly, Caregiver, Professional, and Admin.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, primarily for profile display and communication, not for authentication which is handled externally.

role.description: The assigned role of the user, determining their access and dashboard features.

notificationPreferences.description: JSON string containing user-specific notification settings (e.g., enableEmail, enablePush).

accessibilitySettings.description: JSON string containing user-specific accessibility settings (e.g., fontSize, theme).

caregiverIds.description: References to UserProfile entities with a 'Caregiver' role, associated with this Elderly user. (Relationship: UserProfile (Elderly) N:N UserProfile (Caregiver))

professionalIds.description: References to UserProfile entities with a 'Professional' role, associated with this Elderly user. (Relationship: UserProfile (Elderly) N:N UserProfile (Professional))

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

HealthMetric.title: HealthMetric
HealthMetric.description: Records a specific health measurement or vital sign for an elderly user, providing data for health tracking and management.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HealthMetric entity.

elderlyUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity (role: Elderly) for whom this metric was recorded. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N HealthMetric)

metricType.description: The type of health metric being recorded (e.g., 'bloodPressure', 'heartRate', 'bloodSugar', 'weight', 'temperature').

value.description: The measured value of the health metric (e.g., '120/80' for blood pressure, '72' for heart rate, '110' for blood sugar, '70' for weight, '36.5' for temperature).

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the metric (e.g., 'mmHg', 'bpm', 'mg/dL', 'kg', '°C').

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity (Caregiver, Professional, or Elderly) who recorded this metric. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N HealthMetric)

recordedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the health metric was recorded.

notes.description: Additional notes or observations related to the health metric entry.

Medication.title: Medication
Medication.description: Defines a specific medication prescribed to an elderly user, including dosage and instructions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Medication entity.

name.description: The commercial or generic name of the medication.

dosage.description: The dosage strength of the medication (e.g., '10mg', '5ml').

frequency.description: How often the medication should be taken (e.g., 'Once daily', 'Twice a day with meals').

instructions.description: Specific instructions for taking the medication (e.g., 'Take with food', 'Do not crush').

startDate.description: The date when the medication regimen began.

endDate.description: The date when the medication regimen is expected to end, if applicable.

prescribedByProfessionalId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity (role: Professional) who prescribed this medication. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Medication)

elderlyUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity (role: Elderly) for whom this medication is prescribed. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Medication)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the medication record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the medication record was last updated.

MedicationAdherence.title: MedicationAdherence
MedicationAdherence.description: Tracks and records when an elderly user took a specific dose of their prescribed medication, facilitating adherence monitoring.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicationAdherence entity.

medicationId.description: Reference to the Medication entity for which adherence is being tracked. (Relationship: Medication 1:N MedicationAdherence)

elderlyUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity (role: Elderly) who was prescribed the medication. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MedicationAdherence)

adherenceTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the medication was actually taken or confirmed as taken.

expectedTime.description: The expected timestamp for when the medication should have been taken, used for comparison.

isTaken.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the medication dose was taken.

notes.description: Additional notes regarding medication adherence (e.g., 'Refused', 'Taken late').

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity (Caregiver, Professional, or Elderly) who recorded this adherence entry. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MedicationAdherence)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the adherence record was created.

HealthLog.title: HealthLog
HealthLog.description: General health notes, observations, or updates recorded for an elderly user by caregivers, professionals, or the elderly user themselves.

id.description: Unique identifier for the HealthLog entity.

elderlyUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity (role: Elderly) for whom this log was recorded. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N HealthLog)

logDate.description: Timestamp when the health log entry occurred or was observed.

category.description: The category of the health log (e.g., 'Mood', 'Activity', 'Sleep', 'Symptoms', 'Notes').

details.description: Detailed notes or description of the health log entry.

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity (Caregiver, Professional, or Elderly) who recorded this health log. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N HealthLog)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the health log record was created.

DailyCheckIn.title: DailyCheckIn
DailyCheckIn.description: Records daily well-being status reported by an elderly user, often through a simple questionnaire.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DailyCheckIn entity.

elderlyUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity (role: Elderly) who performed this daily check-in. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N DailyCheckIn)

checkInDate.description: The specific date of the daily check-in.

moodRating.description: A numerical rating of the user's mood for the day (e.g., 1-5 scale).

physicalFeeling.description: A textual description of the user's physical feeling (e.g., 'Good', 'Tired', 'Pain', 'Energetic').

sleptWell.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the user reports having slept well.

notes.description: Any additional qualitative notes from the daily check-in.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the daily check-in record was created.

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a communication message sent between individual users or within a community hub.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Message entity.

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who sent the message. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Message)

receiverId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who is the direct recipient of the message (for private messages). (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Message)

communityId.description: Reference to the Community entity where the message was posted (for public community messages). (Relationship: Community 1:N Message)

content.description: The textual content of the message.

sentAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the message was sent.

isRead.description: Boolean flag indicating whether the message has been read by the primary receiver.

threadId.description: Optional reference to group messages into a conversation thread for better organization.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents a scheduled activity or gathering, facilitating social interaction and community building.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

title.description: The title or name of the event.

description.description: A detailed description of the event, including purpose and agenda.

startTime.description: The start date and time of the event.

endTime.description: The estimated or actual end date and time of the event.

location.description: The physical address or virtual link for the event location.

creatorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who created or organized this event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Event)

communityId.description: Reference to the Community entity this event belongs to, if it's a community-wide event. (Relationship: Community 1:N Event)

attendeeIds.description: References to UserProfile entities who are planning to attend the event. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Event)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the event record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the event record was last updated.

KnowledgeResource.title: KnowledgeResource
KnowledgeResource.description: Stores informational articles, guides, or other helpful resources, forming a knowledge hub for users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the KnowledgeResource entity.

title.description: The title of the knowledge resource.

category.description: The category the resource belongs to (e.g., 'Nutrition', 'Exercise', 'Mental Health', 'Caregiving Tips').

content.description: The main content of the resource, typically in HTML or Markdown format, for display in the app.

authorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity (typically Professional or Admin) who authored or published the resource. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N KnowledgeResource)

publishedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the resource was first published.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the resource was last updated.

tags.description: A list of keywords or tags associated with the resource for improved searchability and categorization.

targetAudience.description: A list of user roles for whom this resource is primarily intended (e.g., 'Elderly', 'Caregiver', 'Professional').

Community.title: Community
Community.description: Represents a social group or hub where users can interact through messaging, shared events, and resources.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Community entity.

name.description: The name of the community group.

description.description: A brief description of the community's purpose or focus.

createdByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who created this community. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Community)

memberIds.description: References to UserProfile entities who are members of this community. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Community)

privacySetting.description: The privacy setting of the community (e.g., 'Public', 'Private', 'Moderated').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the community was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the community was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, including roles (Elderly, Caregiver, Professional), preferences, and metadata. 'userId' matches 'request.auth.uid'. This document also includes 'caregiverIds' and 'professionalIds' for elderly users to manage their network, which are denormalized into their associated health records.

An 'existence-based' collection for global 'Admin' roles. An empty document at this path signifies that 'userId' has admin privileges. This adheres to the DBAC principle of 'Existence over Content' for global roles.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who has the Admin role, matching 'request.auth.uid'.

definition.description: Records health metrics for a specific elderly user. Includes denormalized 'elderlyCaregiverIds' and 'elderlyProfessionalIds' from the parent 'UserProfile' for authorization independence, allowing caregivers/professionals to record/view metrics without 'get()' calls.

params.name: elderlyUserId
params.description: The unique ID of the elderly user to whom the health metric belongs.

params.name: healthMetricId
params.description: The unique ID of the health metric record.

definition.description: Defines prescribed medications for a specific elderly user. Includes denormalized 'elderlyCaregiverIds' and 'elderlyProfessionalIds' from the parent 'UserProfile' for authorization independence.

params.name: elderlyUserId
params.description: The unique ID of the elderly user for whom the medication is prescribed.

params.name: medicationId
params.description: The unique ID of the medication record.

definition.description: Tracks medication adherence for a specific medication prescribed to an elderly user. Includes denormalized 'elderlyCaregiverIds' and 'elderlyProfessionalIds' from the root 'UserProfile' for authorization independence.

params.name: elderlyUserId
params.description: The unique ID of the elderly user.

params.name: medicationId
params.description: The unique ID of the medication.

params.name: adherenceId
params.description: The unique ID of the adherence record.

definition.description: Stores general health notes and observations for a specific elderly user. Includes denormalized 'elderlyCaregiverIds' and 'elderlyProfessionalIds' from the parent 'UserProfile' for authorization independence.

params.name: elderlyUserId
params.description: The unique ID of the elderly user.

params.name: healthLogId
params.description: The unique ID of the health log entry.

definition.description: Records daily well-being status reported by an elderly user. Includes denormalized 'elderlyCaregiverIds' and 'elderlyProfessionalIds' from the parent 'UserProfile' for authorization independence.

params.name: elderlyUserId
params.description: The unique ID of the elderly user.

params.name: checkInId
params.description: The unique ID of the daily check-in record.

definition.description: Stores private messages sent by a user. This collection acts as the sender's 'outbox'. The message document explicitly contains both 'senderId' and 'receiverId' for complete authorization context.

params.name: senderId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who sent the message.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the message.

definition.description: Stores private messages received by a user. This collection acts as the receiver's 'inbox'. The message document explicitly contains both 'senderId' and 'receiverId' for complete authorization context.

params.name: receiverId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who is the recipient of the message.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the message.

definition.description: Represents a social group or hub. The document contains 'memberIds' for authorization checks. These 'memberIds' are denormalized into subcollection documents for Authorization Independence.

params.name: communityId
params.description: The unique ID of the community.

definition.description: Messages posted within a specific community. Includes denormalized 'communityMemberIds' (or a similar members map) from the parent 'Community' document for authorization independence.

params.name: communityId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent community.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique ID of the message.

definition.description: Events scheduled within a specific community. Includes denormalized 'communityMemberIds' (or a similar members map) from the parent 'Community' document for authorization independence.

params.name: communityId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent community.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique ID of the event.

definition.description: Stand-alone events not associated with a specific community. Authorization relies on 'creatorId' and 'attendeeIds' within the document.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique ID of the event.

definition.description: Publicly accessible or role-restricted informational articles and guides. Authorization for reading can be based on the 'targetAudience' field, and writing by 'authorId' or Admin role.

params.name: resourceId
params.description: The unique ID of the knowledge resource.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, QAPs, and Invariants. The primary strategy involves a combination of structural segregation and selective denormalization of authorization context to ensure robust, debuggable, and scalable security rules.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This is achieved by ensuring that all authorization-relevant data is either present within the document being accessed, or within its immediate hierarchical path. Crucially, cross-document `get()` calls for authorization checks in security rules are minimized, especially for documents in subcollections whose access depends on parent attributes. The following denormalization strategies are employed:
1.  **Elderly-Specific Data:** For all subcollections under `/user_profiles/{elderlyUserId}` (e.g., `health_metrics`, `medications`, `adherence_records`, `health_logs`, `daily_check_ins`), each document will include denormalized fields `elderlyCaregiverIds` and `elderlyProfessionalIds`. These arrays will be copied from the associated `UserProfile` document (`/user_profiles/{elderlyUserId}`). This allows rules governing these health-related documents to verify if `request.auth.uid` is the `elderlyUserId` or one of their authorized caregivers/professionals directly from the document itself, without needing a `get()` to the parent `UserProfile` document.
2.  **Community Data (Messages & Events):** For documents in `/communities/{communityId}/messages/{messageId}` and `/communities/{communityId}/events/{eventId}`, each document will include a denormalized `communityMemberIds` array (or a `members` map) copied from its parent `Community` document (`/communities/{communityId}`). This ensures that rules can check if `request.auth.uid` is a member of the community directly from the message or event document, eliminating `get()` calls to the parent community for authorization.
3.  **Private Chat Messages:** For documents in `/private_messages/{senderId}/sent_messages/{messageId}` and `/private_messages/{receiverId}/inbox_messages/{messageId}`, the `senderId` and `receiverId` fields within the message document, combined with the `senderId` or `receiverId` in the path, provide direct authorization context, ensuring independence.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structure supports Queryable Authorization Patterns by segregating data based on its security posture and ownership, enabling secure `list` operations without requiring rules to filter results. Instead, rules authorize the *query itself* based on the context:
1.  **User-Owned Data:** Data like `health_metrics`, `medications`, `health_logs`, `daily_check_ins` are placed under specific user paths (`/user_profiles/{elderlyUserId}/...`). A query initiated by an authorized user on `/user_profiles/{elderlyUserId}/health_metrics` will inherently target only the relevant data, and security rules will authorize the query based on `request.auth.uid` matching `elderlyUserId` or being in the denormalized `elderlyCaregiverIds`/`elderlyProfessionalIds` fields. The rule does not filter *which* metrics are returned, but rather *if* the user is allowed to query *any* metrics for that `elderlyUserId`.
2.  **Community Data:** Community messages and events are nested under `/communities/{communityId}/...`. Queries on these subcollections are scoped to a specific community, and rules check `request.auth.uid` against the denormalized `communityMemberIds` in the queried documents, ensuring only community members can perform these queries. This allows `list` operations to return all relevant items within the authorized scope.
3.  **Private Messages:** By creating dedicated 'inbox' and 'sent_messages' subcollections under each user's path, `list` operations for a user's messages are naturally filtered by the path itself (e.g., querying `/user_profiles/{userId}/inbox_messages` only shows messages *for* `userId`). Rules simply verify `request.auth.uid` matches the `userId` in the path.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
*   **Private User Data:** `UserProfile` data is stored in `/user_profiles/{userId}`. Personal and sensitive data, like health metrics and medication adherence, are deeply nested under `/user_profiles/{elderlyUserId}/...` to leverage path-based ownership for `elderlyUserId`. 
*   **Global Roles (DBAC):** For the 'Admin' role, existence-based authorization is used via a dedicated collection `/admin_roles/{userId}`. Other roles (Elderly, Caregiver, Professional) are stored directly within the `UserProfile` document.
*   **Collaborative Data:** `Community` entities and their nested `Message` and `Event` subcollections utilize a membership-map/array (`memberIds`, `communityMemberIds`) for controlled access.
*   **Global Resources:** `KnowledgeResource` entities are in a top-level collection (`/knowledge_resources/{resourceId}`), where access can be public or role-based, enforced by the `targetAudience` field in the document and the user's `UserProfile.role` (which can be checked via `get()` on `request.auth.uid`'s own profile).
